name: F.F.E.
description_short: F.F.E. Federal Credit Union is a small federal credit union in Fargo, ND serving government employees with basic banking services since 1954.
description_long: F.F.E. Federal Credit Union was established in 1954 and has operated for 72 years as a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Fargo, North Dakota. The institution serves a membership base of approximately 446 members and maintains a single branch location at 657 2nd Ave N in downtown Fargo. As a credit union, F.F.E. operates under federal charter number 9102 and serves members primarily from federal, state, and local government employment sectors. F.F.E. offers core banking products including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various lending services. The credit union maintains a loan portfolio of approximately $675,000 across unsecured loans and other loan categories. Members have access to low-cost wire transfer services and traditional credit union financial products. The institution does not function as a corporate credit union and maintains NCUSIF deposit insurance coverage for member protections. F.F.E. distinguishes itself through its specialized membership focus on government employees and its long operational history serving the Fargo community. As a small, locally-rooted institution, it provides personalized service compared to larger regional or national banks. The credit union leadership includes CEO Lacey A Onsurez and Chairperson Jay Liversage, reflecting stable governance over its 72-year tenure. However, prospective members should note that F.F.E. is an exceptionally small institution with only $3.56 million in total assets and experienced a net loss of $60 in the most recent reporting period (as of May 2025). The credit union maintains zero investment holdings and no real estate loans, indicating limited product sophistication. Membership eligibility is restricted to government employees, and the single branch location limits accessibility. The institution's financial condition warrants careful evaluation before joining.
pros: Federal credit union with NCUSIF insurance protection for member deposits
72-year operational history demonstrates institutional stability in the Fargo market
Membership restricted to government employees provides community focus and alignment of member interests
Offers ATM/debit card access and low-cost wire transfer services
Personalized service typical of small credit unions versus large impersonal banks
Leadership continuity with established CEO and Board Chairperson
Checking and savings account options with member-owned structure
cons: Exceptionally small institution with only $3.56 million in assets limits service offerings and financial strength
Net loss of $60 in most recent audit period (May 2025) raises concerns about financial sustainability
Single branch location in Fargo provides no geographic flexibility or convenience beyond downtown area
Zero investment portfolio and no real estate lending indicates limited lending capacity and product diversity
Membership eligibility restricted to federal, state, and local government employees only
best_for: Federal, state, or local government employees in the Fargo area seeking basic banking services
Members prioritizing community-focused, locally-rooted credit union relationships over national institutions
Individuals comfortable with minimal branch access and limited product offerings in exchange for personalized service
diagnosis: F.F.E. Federal Credit Union is best suited for government employees in Fargo seeking straightforward checking and savings services from a community-focused institution. The primary caveat is the institution's very small size ($3.56M assets), recent net losses, and restricted membership eligibility, which limits its viability as a primary banking option for most consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
